question: How do I apply to NUST?
answer: To apply to NUST undergraduate programs: 1) Register online at ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk. 2) Fill the online application form and upload your photograph. 3) Print the fee invoice and deposit the processing fee (Rs. 5,000 per NET exam) at any HBL branch. 4) Select your NET test date and session. 5) Appear in the NET test (computer-based, held in multiple sessions between November and July). 6) Your best NET score from multiple attempts is used. 7) Merit lists are generated by last week of July. 8) Selected candidates pay admission dues and submit documents.

question: What is the NET test?
answer: The NUST Entry Test (NET) is a computer-based mandatory entrance exam for NUST undergraduate admissions. It is conducted in multiple sessions from November to July each year in Islamabad, Karachi, Quetta, and Gilgit. You can appear multiple times and your best score is considered. The test is based on intermediate-level education. Registration fee is Rs. 5,000 per attempt. The test covers different subjects depending on your program choice.

The test is based on intermediate-level education. Registration fee is Rs. 5,000 per attempt. The test covers different subjects depending on your program choice.

question: What is the NET test pattern for engineering?
answer: NET-Engineering paper (for Pre-Engineering/equivalent students): Mathematics 40%, Physics 30%, Chemistry 15%, English 10%, Intelligence 5%. This test is for all Engineering and Computing programmes. Candidates seeking engineering admission can also opt for BS Mathematics, BS Physics, BS Chemistry, and BS Food Science & Technology without additional fee.

question: What is the aggregate formula for NUST?
answer: The aggregate formula for all NUST UG programmes (except MBBS) is: NUST Entry Test (NET) 75% + SSC/O-Level/Equivalent 10% + HSSC/A-Level/Equivalent 15%. For O/A Level candidates who are in A Level final year, O Level equivalence marks get 25% weightage. For MBBS: NUMS MDCAT 50% + HSSC/A-Level 40% + SSC/O-Level 10%.

question: What is the merit criteria for NUST?
answer: Merit at NUST is calculated as: 75% NET score + 15% HSSC/FSc/A-Level marks + 10% SSC/Matric/O-Level marks. Merit lists are generated tentatively by the last week of July. Candidates are offered admission based on their merit position and program preferences. You can appear in multiple NET attempts and your best score is used. After merit list announcement, candidates get a chance to review their program preferences.

question: What are the eligibility requirements for engineering at NUST?
answer: For Engineering programmes: You need SSC with Science group AND HSSC Pre-Engineering group (or Computer Science group or Pre-Medical with additional Maths). For O/A Level: O level equivalence of Science group AND A level equivalence of Pre-Engineering group including Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry. Minimum 60% marks in both SSC and HSSC/equivalent. Must appear in NET test. Computer Science group students must clear Chemistry as remedial subject in 1st semester.

question: Does NUST offer scholarships?
answer: Yes, NUST offers extensive financial aid: 1) Need-based scholarships through the NEED Initiative (NUST Endowment for Educational Development) providing half/full tuition fee waivers. Apply by filling the NUST Financial Aid Application Form (NFAAF) with your admission application. 2) Merit-based financial assistance for top 3 position holders per section with GPA 3.5+ per semester. 3) Over 86 endowment fund scholarships from organizations like HBL, UBL, Fauji Cement, Lucky Cement, Netsol, etc. 4) External scholarships: HEC Ehsaas, PEEF, and others. 5) Deferred payment facility for financially challenged students. 6) Ihsaan Trust interest-free loans covering 50-80% tuition.

question: Can I apply to NUST with A-Levels?
answer: Yes, A-Level students can apply to NUST. Requirements: 1) Get O Level and A Level equivalence certificates from IBCC (Inter Boards Coordination Commission) with minimum 60% marks. 2) Valid O Level equivalence is mandatory at time of applying. A Level equivalence must be provided before program commencement. 3) Appear in NET test or apply via ACT/SAT scores. 4) A-Level candidates in final year get 25% weightage to O Level equivalence marks for merit calculation. IBCC office: Plot 25, Street 38, G-10/4, Islamabad. Phone: 051-9106631.

question: Can I apply to NUST with SAT or ACT?
answer: Yes. NUST has reserved ACT/SAT based seats for both national and international students. Requirements: Engineering/Computing/Architecture/Applied Sciences: ACT Composite Score minimum 25. Natural Sciences (Math/Physics/Chemistry): ACT STEM Score minimum 25. Business Studies/Social Sciences/LLB: SAT-I minimum 550 in each subject OR ACT minimum 25 in Math, English, and Reading. Scores must reach NUST by July 25, 2025. NUST SAT code: 2790, ACT code: 2915. Scores are valid for 2 years.

question: Where is NUST located?
answer: NUST main campus is in Sector H-12, Islamabad, Pakistan, in the backdrop of Margalla Hills. It is 20 minutes from the airport, bus and railway terminals via Kashmir Highway connected to M-1 Motorway. NUST has campuses in 5 cities: Islamabad (main H-12 campus with 11 schools), Rawalpindi (MCS and CoEME), Risalpur (MCE and CAE), Karachi (PNEC), and Quetta (NBC - Balochistan Campus).

question: What is the fee refund policy at NUST?
answer: NUST tuition fee refund policy: Full (100%) refund if you apply within 7 days of classes starting. Half (50%) refund from 8th to 15th day of classes. No refund (0%) from 16th day onwards. The Admission Processing Fee is non-refundable under any circumstances. Security Deposit is refundable but must be claimed within 3 years of degree completion/withdrawal. Late payment penalty: 2% of semester fee after 1st due date; registration suspended after 2nd due date.

question: Can DAE holders apply to NUST?
answer: Yes, candidates with Diploma of Associate Engineering (DAE) can apply to NUST. They need minimum 60% marks in DAE in various technologies and can apply for relevant Engineering programmes through the NUST Entry Test (NET) on open merit. DAE candidates cannot opt for BS Mathematics, BS Physics, or BS Chemistry alongside engineering. For details visit: nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/dae-eligibility-criteria/

question: When does the academic year start at NUST?
answer: The NUST academic year starts in September. There are two regular semesters: Fall (September-January) and Spring (February-June), plus an optional Summer semester (July-August). Summer semester allows students to clear deficient courses. The university observes summer vacation from August to September. NUST operates 9am to 5pm, 5 days a week. Labs and libraries remain accessible till late night and weekends.

question: Can I transfer to NUST from another university?
answer: Yes, NUST allows migration from other universities. Requirements: Apply after completing 1st year at parent university. Need SSC and HSSC with minimum 60% marks. Courses to transfer must have minimum B grade. Must complete at least 60% credit hours at NUST. Students who previously failed NUST entry test are not eligible. Fees: Processing Fee Rs. 7,000. Migration from foreign universities Rs. 250,000. From local universities Rs. 100,000. Transfer within NUST Rs. 7,000.

question: How can I join NUST?
answer: To join NUST for undergraduate programs: 1) Register online at ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk. 2) Pay the registration fee of Rs. 5,000 at any HBL branch. 3) Appear in the NUST Entry Test (NET) which is held multiple times from November to July. 4) You can also apply via ACT/SAT scores. 5) Merit lists are generated by last week of July based on: 75% NET + 15% FSc + 10% Matric. 6) Selected candidates pay admission dues and join in September.

To join NUST for undergraduate programs: 1) Register online at ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk. 2) Pay the registration fee of Rs. 5,000 at any HBL branch. 3) Appear in the NUST Entry Test (NET) which is held multiple times from November to July. 4) You can also apply via ACT/SAT scores. 5) Merit lists are generated by last week of July based on: 75% NET + 15% FSc + 10% Matric. 6) Selected candidates pay admission dues and join in September.

question: What are the ways to get admission in NUST?
answer: There are two main routes for NUST UG admission: 1) NUST Entry Test (NET) — computer-based test held in multiple sessions. Apply at ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk. Fee: Rs. 5,000 per attempt. Best score from multiple attempts is used. 2) ACT/SAT — reserved seats available. Engineering/Computing need ACT Composite minimum 25. Business/Social Sciences need SAT minimum 550 per subject. Scores must reach NUST by July 25. Both routes use the same merit formula: 75% test + 15% FSc + 10% Matric.

question: Can I get into NUST without NET?
answer: Yes, you can apply to NUST via ACT or SAT scores instead of NET. For Engineering/Computing: ACT Composite minimum 25. For Natural Sciences: ACT STEM minimum 25. For Business/Social Sciences/LLB: SAT-I minimum 550 per subject OR ACT minimum 25 in Math, English, Reading. SAT code: 2790, ACT code: 2915. Scores must reach NUST by July 25, 2025. Separate merit list is prepared for ACT/SAT candidates.

question: What is LID at NUST?
answer: LID likely refers to the Bachelor of Industrial Design (B.ID) offered at SADA (School of Art, Design and Architecture) at NUST. It is a 4-year undergraduate program. Eligibility: SSC and HSSC with Mathematics and Physics as mandatory subjects, minimum 60% marks. Admission is through NET-Architecture paper (Math 30%, Physics 30%, English 25%, Intelligence 15%) or ACT Composite Score (minimum 25). The tuition fee is Rs. 250,380 per semester.

question: Can a candidate of ICS / equivalent qualification eligible to apply for Engineering programmes?
answer: Yes, as per PEC regulation No PEC/EAD/46-VCCM/Regulations/2023 dated 30 March 2023, candidates having combination of Physics, Mathematics and Computer Studies / Computer Science (ICS) or equivalent are eligible to apply for all the Engineering programs offered by NUST, but they have to clear Chemistry as a remedial subject / course in the 1st Semester after admission. Candidates, who already appeared in any NET series during current admission cycle, will also be able to update their choice preferences while applying for Series-4 or after the announcement of merit, before start of the selection process.

question: Can I apply for rechecking of Entry Test Result?

question: I am a born Pakistani with a foreign passport; can I apply as an expatriate student?
answer: If you don't have Pakistani nationality like having CNIC / NICOP, you can apply as an international student (foreign student). You can apply on the basis of NET or ACT/SAT or both as per eligibility criteria but have to pay tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for foreign nationals. If you are a POC holder and applying on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT national seat, you will be paying tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for national student.

question: What category of candidates can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT?
answer: Any candidate can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT provided he / she has score for the prescribed disciplines of study. Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for this purpose. Admission on ACT / SAT basis has two categories, i.e National and International. Candidates admitted on National seats pay tuition fee and allied charges in Pak currency whereas candidates admitted against International seats pay the required dues at rates defined for international students. For admission against international seats, the following categories will be eligible:- Pakistani national students having passed an examination equivalent to intermediate level of Pakistan from a foreign education system. Foreigners and Pakistanis having dual nationality, irrespective of the place of their HSSC study, i.e Pakistan or abroad. Pakistani students who studied abroad but have passed the requisite examination from a Pakistani board.

question: What are the salient features of Admission System of NUST?
answer: Admission is granted on the basis of candidates’ performance in NUST Entry Test (NET) or score in Scholastic Aptitude Test (SAT) for which limited number of seats are reserved. The entire process of registration for admission is online. Candidates must be in possession of CNIC or Form B or NICOP (National Identity Card for Overseas Pakistanis). For applying for Registration:- FA / FSc stream candidates should have passed Part-I exam. A level students should have obtained minimum 60% marks in O level as per equivalence cirtificate issued by IBCC. When applications for admissions are invited, candidates are first required to get registered by providing valid email address. On provision of registration number, the candidate is required to fill up the online admission form and deposit specified processing fee. Candidates can appear in the entry test more than once to improve their score. The merit for admission is prepared on the basis of best score. For every attempt to appear in NET, a separate application is to be filled up. Computer based entry tests for all disciplines are held in multiple series at NUST Campus Islamabad according to the following schedule: First Series (NET-1) - 7 - 21 Dec 2019 Second Series (NET-2) - 14 Mar 2020 onwards Third Series (NET-3) - Jun-Jul 2020 Paper based entry test is conducted in Karachi and Quetta in Mar & July. In NET-2 & 3, candidates have an option either to appear for Paper Based Test at Karachi & Quetta or Computer Based Test at Islamabad. In Computer-based Entry Test, candidates can select the date and session of test according to their choice depending on availability of space. Admission process for all undergraduate programmes is conducted once a year to facilitate the commencement of the academic year in Fall. For more details, please visit NUST website www.nust.edu.pk. FAQs Categories

question: Q. I have submitted NFAAF and attached soft copies of supporting documents too, do I need to send hard copies of supporting documents? If yes, When?
answer: ​​ You do not need to send supporting documents to FAO immediately after submitting NFAAF. After due processing, FAO will inform you via email to mail the documents in hard form, within a due date. Failure to provide documents within due date will lead to cancellation of NFAAF. Q. I want to apply for​ other need based scholarships like PEEF, CMEEF KPK, FEF etc. Do I need to submit another application form?​​ Upon submitting NFAAF, you will be considered for all types of need based scholarships. FAO will inform you incase any additional form / document is required.

question: Q. I do not have few of the supporting documents. What should i do?
answer: OR I need more time to arrange the supporting documents. Please allow me to submit NFAAF and provide supporting documents in a few more working days.​​​ You may attach scan copy of a hand written letter of explanation​ (duly signed by you and your parent / guardian). Briefly explain reason of not attaching the supporting document and expected date till you will provide the same to Financial Aid Office. Q. I am trying to upload soft copy of the supporting documents but the system is not saving them. What should i do?​​ File size of each of the supporting document should be less than 250 kb. Resize the scan copy of supporting document and try uploading the same. If problem persists, you may forward a screen shot of the error to [email protected] . Q. I have been offered admission in two or more disciplines and have NOT been offered scholarship in ALL of these disciplines. Is this provisional scholarship offer valid for only specific disciplines?​​​ Scholarship offered is valid for ALL disciplines offered admission in a particular selection list e.g if you secure admission in BS Physics, Chemistry & Maths in the 1st selection list, provisional admission letters wi​ll have the same scholarship offer. If you have NOT been offered scholarship in any of the disciplines, please write to us at [email protected] . Q. I have secured admission (for example in BS Civil Engineering in 1st selection list) and have been offered need based scholarship. Will this scholarship be transferred to other discipline(s) I am offered admission in subsequent selection list(s). How will the scholarship be transferred to other disciplines? What happens to the scholarship offer if I don’t pay dues now and wait for securing admission in subsequent lists? ​​ Scholarship is awarded on merit cum need basis i.e on the basis of a candidates’ position on the financial need scale in a particular selection list. Scholarship once offered to you may be transferred to ALL other offered disciplines, only if you have already deposited dues to secure admission / scholarship i.e for BS Civil Engineering in 1st selection list in the example above. To transfer scholarship offer to other disciplines, you will be required to give willingness to admission office ( [email protected] , [email protected] ). Scholarship offered to you- for BS Civil Engg in the example above- will be cancelled incase you do not deposit dues, and vacant slot will be offered in the subsequent selection list on merit cum need basis. Q. My Financial Profile Has Changed After Submission Of NFAAF. How Can I Make Changes In NFAAF After Submission? OR ​I Have Made Mistakes In The NFAAF. I Cannot Access It After Submission. How Can I Get It Corrected?​​ You will be required to inform FAO via email ( [email protected] ) specifically mentioning the following: Section of NFAAF. Sub section of NFAAF. Title of the Field(s) to be amended. Amended​ Values. If necessary, someone from the FAO may contact you on contact numbers (provided in the NFAAF) to acquire additional information from you / family members. ​ Q. What Happens If My Parents Are Divorced, Separated And One Parent Is Unwilling To Submit Any Information To The Financial Aid Office Or To Contribute Towards My NUST Expenses?​​​ ​ ​In fairness to all, NUST expects that parents will pay what they can, and we must assess each parent’s ability to contribute to college costs by reviewing complete financial documents from both parents. You might explain to the parent, unwilling to submit financial information, that his or her information will remain confidential. Your parent should also know that providing such information no way obligates him or her to contribute to your NUST education. If you believe your situation is special, please write us a detailed letter of explanation.​ Q. May I Send The Deed Of Agreement In The Same Envelope In Which I Am Sending Admission Documents?​​​ Deed of agreement is to be forwarded to Financial Aid Office separately​. Q. What Is The Need Assessment Process?​​ The decision and its review follow the stages outlined below: The staff at the financial aid office (FAO) checks and reconciles the information on the financial aid form against the documents provided including their tenor and validity; seeking clarifications from the applicant/ student on anomalies, if any. Applicants are contacted for retrieval of discrepant documents (where necessary). FAO prepares student evaluation sheets that contain a brief summary of the credentials provided covering all the aspects to be presented to the financial aid committee for final decision. Shortlisted Candidates are informed to submit supporting documentation in hard form by the deadline specified by the financial aid office. Interviews / Physical Verifications are conducted where recommended by the committee. The NUST financial aid committee assesses each case through the evaluation sheet and reviews supporting documents before making their decision which is purely based on need. Decision is communicated to the students through an official provisional scholarship award letter. The Financial Aid Office reserves the right to conduct periodic reviews to ensure the award is granted to the needy students only. Q. My Father / Guardian / Earning Member Of The Family Does Not Have A Job. What Type Of Income Certificate Should I Give?​​ Father / Guardian / Earning Member(s) of the Family will give a Declaration of monthly income from all sources on affidavit to be printed on stamp paper having value > Rs.100, duly attested by Union Councilor & two witnesses. At least one of the witnesses is to be a Govt. officer of BPS 17 or above & the other a close family member well acquainted with your family financial profile. Contact numbers of the witnesses to be mentioned on the affidavit. Q. My Father / Guardian Does Business. What Type Of Income Certificate Should I Give?​​ Father / Guardian / Earning Member(s) of the Family will give a Declaration of monthly income from all sources (business) on affidavit to be printed on stamp paper having value > Rs.100, duly attested by Union Councilor & two witnesses. At least one of the witnesses is to be a Govt. officer of BPS 17 or above & the other a close family member well acquainted with your family financial profile. Contact numbers of the witnesses to be mentioned on the affidavit. Q. My Father / Guardian Does Job And Has A Part Time Business (Agriculture, Taxi, Tuition, Shop Etc). What Type Of Income Certificate Should I Give?​​ Father / Guardian / Earning Member(s) of the Family will give salary certificate and a Declaration of monthly income from all sources (job & Business) on affidavit to be printed on stamp paper having value > Rs.100, duly attested by Union Councilor & two witnesses. At least one of the witnesses is to be a Govt. officer of BPS 17 or above & the other a close family member well acquainted with your family financial profile. Contact numbers of the witnesses to be mentioned on the affidavit. Q. Our Monthly Income Is From Agricultural Lands. What Type Of Income Certificate Should I Give?​​​ Father / guardian will a Declaration of monthly income​ from all sources on affidavit to be printed on stamp paper having value > Rs.100, duly attested by Assistant Commissioner Revenue / Union Councilor & two witnesses. At least one of the witnesses is to be a Govt. officer of BPS 17 or above & the other a close family member well acquainted with your family financial profile. Contact numbers of the witnesses to be mentioned on the affidavit. Q. I Was Awarded A Scholarship From A Private Outside Organization. Do I Need To Inform Financial Aid Office?​​ Yes, if you receive an outside grant or scholarship or a third-party payment you must report the type and amount to the FAO as and when received. Q. Can I Apply F​Or The Financial Aid During The Semester/Year?​​ Only those students, whose financial situation changes drastically, due to unforeseen circumstances e.g. Retirement, death, layoffs from job etc. can apply for financial assistance during the semester/ year through their institutions.​ Q. What Is The Criteria Of Awarding Financial Aid?​​ The scholarship management committee assesses financial need of all candidates and determines whether or not candidates family has sufficient resources to afford fee at NUST. The need is determined on the basis of the financial credentials submitted by the candidate. Q. Can a student appeal for review of the financial assistance decision?​​ There is an appeal process in place in which the NUST administration accepts requests from students who have additional information to declare, which may have been missed during the initial assessment. question: What is BSAI at NUST?
answer: BSAI stands for BS Artificial Intelligence, offered at SEECS (School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science) at NUST H-12 Islamabad. It is a 4-year undergraduate program. The tuition fee is Rs. 171,350 per semester (same as all engineering/computing programs). Eligibility: SSC with Science group AND HSSC Pre-Engineering or Computer Science or Pre-Medical group, minimum 60% marks.
content: What is BSAI at NUST? BSAI BS Artificial Intelligence program SEECS undergraduate 4 year tuition fee Rs 171350 per semester. What does BSAI stand for? AI program at NUST.

question: What is GNET?
answer: GNET stands for Graduate NUST Entry Test. It is an alternative to the GAT (General) test for MS/Masters program admission at NUST. GNET requires a minimum score of 50 (accumulative). NUST accepts GNET scores conducted on or after June 2023. You can register for GNET online on the NUST website.
content: What is GNET? GNET Graduate NUST Entry Test alternative to GAT for MS Masters admission. Minimum score 50. Register online NUST website. What does GNET stand for?

question: What can pre-medical students study at NUST?
answer: Pre-Medical (FSc Pre-Medical) students can apply for: 1) BS Biotechnology, BS Environmental Science, BS Agriculture, BS Food Science and Technology at ASAB/SCEE. 2) Computing programs (BSCS, BS Data Science, BS Bioinformatics, BS AI) - but must clear deficient Mathematics courses of 6 credit hours within one year. 3) Engineering programs - but must clear the condensed Mathematics course of 8 weeks offered by NUST prior to admission. 4) BBA, BS Economics, BS Psychology, and other Business/Social Sciences programs (no restrictions). 5) MBBS at NSHS.

Biotechnology Environmental Science Agriculture Food Science BSCS BS Data Science BS AI computing programs clear mathematics deficient courses. Engineering condensed math course. BBA Economics Psychology business social sciences. MBBS.

question: How many types of financial aid does NUST offer?
answer: NUST offers three types of financial aid: (1) Need-based scholarships — full or half tuition fee waivers based on family income via the NEED Initiative (NUST Endowment for Educational Development). (2) Merit-based financial assistance — for students who maintain a minimum GPA of 3.5 per semester and finish in the top 3 positions in their section. (3) Interest-free loans — through the Ihsan Trust, covering 50–80% of tuition.
content: How many types of financial aid does NUST offer? NUST offers three types of financial aid: (1) Need-based scholarships — full or half tuition fee waivers based on family income via the NEED Initiative (NUST Endowment for Educational Development). (2) Merit-based financial assistance — for students who maintain a minimum GPA of 3.5 per semester and finish in the top 3 positions in their section. (3) Interest-free loans — through the Ihsan Trust, covering 50–80% of tuition.

question: When and how do I apply for financial aid at NUST?
answer: UG candidates apply for financial aid immediately after submitting their online admission application. You must fill the NUST Need-Based Financial Aid Application Form (NFAAF) online via the admission portal. The NFAAF deadline is the same as the admission application deadline — tentatively June for UG. Financial aid applications are not accepted separately after the due date. For MS, the deadline is tentatively May.

question: Who can apply for financial aid at NUST?
answer: All UG and Masters candidates are eligible to apply for financial aid at the time of admission. There is no restriction based on program or school — if you are applying for admission, you can simultaneously apply for financial aid.

question: Does applying for financial aid affect my NUST admission application?
answer: No. Applying for financial aid has absolutely no effect on your admission decision. Even if you leave the NFAAF form unfilled or partially filled, it will not affect whether you get admission. The admission and financial aid processes are completely independent.

question: What is physical verification for NUST financial aid?
answer: Physical verification means the NUST Financial Aid Office (FAO) team may visit your home, business premises, or inquire from neighbors to verify the facts you stated in your financial aid application. This can happen at any point during your academic life at NUST, not just at the time of initial application.

question: Do I need to send hard copies of documents after submitting NFAAF?
answer: No, you do not need to send hard copies of supporting documents immediately after submitting the NFAAF. After processing your application, the Financial Aid Office (FAO) will email you with instructions to mail hard copy documents by a specified deadline. Failure to provide documents by that deadline will result in cancellation of your NFAAF.

question: Do I need a separate form to apply for PEEF or other external scholarships at NUST?
answer: No. Once you submit the NFAAF, NUST automatically considers you for all types of need-based scholarships including PEEF, CMEEF (KPK), FEF, and others. The FAO will inform you if any additional form or document is needed for a specific scholarship.

question: What if I don't have some of the required documents for the NUST financial aid form?
answer: If you are missing some supporting documents, you can attach a handwritten letter of explanation (signed by you and your parent/guardian) stating the reason for the missing document and the expected date by which you will provide it to the Financial Aid Office. If the system won't let you upload, ensure each document file is under 250KB. For upload errors, email financialaid@nust.edu.pk with a screenshot.

question: Can my NUST scholarship be transferred if I change my program from the merit list?
answer: Yes, a scholarship offer can be transferred to other programs you are offered in subsequent selection lists, but only if you have already deposited dues to secure admission in your first offered program. To transfer, notify the admission office at ugadmissions@nust.edu.pk and fao@nust.edu.pk. If you do not deposit dues, the scholarship offer is cancelled and the slot is offered to others in the next list.

question: How do I correct or update my NFAAF after submission?
answer: After submitting your NFAAF, you cannot directly edit it. To make changes, email fao@nust.edu.pk specifying: the Section, Sub-section, Field title to be amended, and the corrected values. The FAO may contact you for additional information. If your financial profile changes significantly, you can apply for a review at any time through your institution.

If your financial profile changes significantly, you can apply for a review at any time through your institution.

question: My parents are divorced. Will that affect my NUST financial aid application?
answer: NUST expects both parents to provide financial information even if they are separated or divorced. Both parents' financial documents are assessed to determine contribution ability. If one parent is unwilling, inform them that their information stays confidential and does not obligate them to pay. If your situation is special, write a detailed letter of explanation to the Financial Aid Office.

question: What is the need assessment process for NUST financial aid?
answer: The NUST need assessment process follows these stages: (1) FAO verifies NFAAF and supporting documents, seeks clarifications on anomalies. (2) Applicants are contacted to resolve discrepancies. (3) FAO prepares evaluation sheets for each applicant. (4) Shortlisted candidates submit hard-copy documents. (5) Interviews and physical verification are conducted where necessary. (6) The NUST Financial Aid Committee reviews each case and decides based purely on financial need. (7) Decision is communicated via an official provisional scholarship award letter. FAO conducts periodic reviews to ensure the award reaches genuinely needy students.

question: What income certificate do I need for NUST financial aid if my father is unemployed or does business?
answer: For the NUST NFAAF income certificate requirements: (1) If unemployed: a Declaration of all income sources on affidavit on stamp paper (>Rs. 100), attested by Union Councilor and two witnesses (one a Govt. officer BPS-17+). (2) If business: same affidavit format listing all business income. (3) If salaried + part-time (agriculture, shop, taxi, tuition): salary certificate plus affidavit for supplemental income. (4) If agricultural income: affidavit attested by Assistant Commissioner Revenue or Union Councilor and two witnesses.

question: If I receive a scholarship from an outside organization, should I tell NUST?
answer: Yes. If you receive any external grant, scholarship, or third-party payment, you must report the type and amount to the NUST Financial Aid Office (FAO) as soon as you receive it. Failure to report may affect your NUST financial aid status.

question: Can I apply for NUST financial aid during the semester or academic year?
answer: Generally, financial aid applications are only accepted at the time of admission. However, students whose financial situation drastically changes during the year due to unforeseen circumstances (e.g., parent's death, retirement, job loss) can apply for financial assistance during the semester or year through their institution.

question: What is the criteria for awarding financial aid at NUST?
answer: The NUST scholarship committee assesses the financial need of all applicants to determine whether their family has sufficient resources to afford NUST fees. Need is determined purely based on the financial credentials submitted (income certificates, bank statements, property details, etc.). The process is transparent — there is also an appeal process where students can provide additional information that may have been missed during initial assessment.

question: What is the fee for Engineering and Computing programs (BSCS, SE, Mechanical, Civil, Electrical, AI, Data Science)?
answer: The tuition fee for all Engineering and Computing programs at NUST is Rs. 171,350 per semester. Additionally, there is a one-time Admission Processing Fee of Rs. 35,000 (non-refundable), a Security Deposit of Rs. 10,000 (refundable), and Miscellaneous dues of Rs. 2,700 per semester. For 4 years (8 semesters), the total estimated tuition cost is around Rs. 1.4 Million.

question: What is the fee for Business Studies, Social Sciences (S3H), Architecture, and LLB? (BBA, ACF, Economics, etc.)
answer: The tuition fee for Business (BBA, ACF), Social Sciences (Economics, Psychology, Public Admin, Mass Comm), Architecture, and LLB is Rs. 250,380 per semester. LLB students also pay a Rs. 3,000 Bar Council Registration fee. There is a one-time Admission Processing Fee of Rs. 35,000, Security Deposit of Rs. 10,000, and Miscellaneous dues of Rs. 2,700 per semester.

question: What is the fee for BS English?
answer: The tuition fee for BS English (Language and Literature) is Rs. 85,000 per semester. One-time admission and security fees also apply.

question: What are the hostel charges and mess fees at NUST?
answer: Hostel charges per month: Single with attached bath (Rs. 11,000), Double with attached bath (Rs. 10,000), Double with community bath (Rs. 8,000), Triple with community bath (Rs. 6,750). The mess fee (food) is approximately Rs. 15,175 per month.
source: Structured Extraction from Official NUST Prospectus 2025

question: What is the merit or aggregate criteria formula for NUST admission?
answer: The NUST aggregate merit formula is: 75% NUST Entry Test (NET) score + 15% HSSC / A-Level / Equivalent score + 10% SSC / O-Level / Equivalent score.
source: Structured Extraction from Official NUST Prospectus 2025

question: Can I request a re-checking of my NET result? What is the process and fee?
answer: Yes, NUST allows candidates to request re-checking of their NET answer paper. The process: submit a written request to the NUST Admissions Directorate within 7 days of result announcement. Fee: Rs. 500 per paper (non-refundable). Contact: admissions@nust.edu.pk or +92-51-9085-6878. The result after re-checking is final.
content: Can I request a re-checking of my NET result? Yes, NUST allows candidates to request re-checking of their NET answer paper. Fee: Rs. 500 per paper. Submit request within 7 days of result. Contact admissions@nust.edu.pk.

question: If I am selected for multiple programs, can I choose which one to accept?
answer: Yes. NUST admission is preference-based. When you fill the admission form, you list your program preferences in order (1st choice, 2nd choice, etc.). NUST offers admission based on merit and your highest available preference. If selected for multiple programs, you are automatically assigned your highest-priority preference for which your merit qualifies. You can update your preferences after the merit list is announced.
content: If selected for multiple programs at NUST, can I choose? Yes. NUST uses a preference-based system. You list programs in order of priority and are assigned your highest qualifying preference. Preferences can be updated after merit list announcement.

question: What documents do I need to upload with my online application? Do I need to get them attested?
answer: Documents required for NUST online application: Scanned copy of CNIC/B-Form, Matriculation (SSC) certificate and marksheet, Inter part-1 marksheet (for ongoing students) or full HSSC result, Domicile certificate, Passport-sized photograph. Documents do NOT need to be attested for the online upload phase. Original attested documents are only required during physical verification at the time of admission confirmation.
content: What documents do I need for NUST online application? CNIC/B-Form, SSC certificate and marksheet, HSSC Part-1 result or full result, domicile, passport photo. Attestation is NOT required for online upload — only for physical verification at admission.

question: What is IBCC and why do I need it?
answer: IBCC (Inter Board Committee of Chairmen) is the government body that provides equivalence certificates for O-Level and A-Level qualifications in Pakistan. If you studied O/A Levels (Cambridge/Edexcel), you MUST obtain an IBCC equivalence certificate to apply to NUST. The certificate converts your grades into a Pakistani percentage. Without it, your application cannot be processed. IBCC office: Plot 25, Street 38, G-10/4, Islamabad. Phone: 051-9106631. Website: ibcc.edu.pk.
content: What is IBCC? IBCC gives equivalence certificates to O/A Level students. Mandatory for Cambridge/Edexcel students applying to NUST. Converts grades to Pakistani percentage. IBCC office in G-10/4 Islamabad. Phone: 051-9106631.

question: What is NUST?
answer: NUST (National University of Sciences and Technology) is Pakistan's #1 ranked university, consistently ranked #1 in Pakistan and in the top 350 globally (QS World Rankings 2025: #353). Established in 1991, it is a public research university with campuses across Pakistan, headquartered at H-12, Islamabad. It offers UG, MS, and PhD programs in Engineering, Computing, Business, Architecture, Law, Medicine, Social Sciences, and Natural Sciences across 21 institutions.
content: What is NUST? NUST (National University of Sciences and Technology) is Pakistan's #1 ranked public research university, QS ranked #353 globally (2025). Est. 1991. H-12 Islamabad campus. 21+ institutions. UG/MS/PhD in Engineering, Computing, Business, Medicine.

question: Is hostel accommodation guaranteed for first-year students?
answer: No, hostel accommodation is NOT guaranteed for first-year students at NUST. Hostel seats are limited and allocated on a first-come, first-served basis after admission confirmation. Many first-year students find accommodation in private hostels or apartments in Islamabad/Rawalpindi, particularly in areas like I-8, G-10, and sectors near H-12. You can apply for NUST hostel through the student portal after admission confirmation — seats are allocated based on availability and distance from home city.
content: Is NUST hostel guaranteed for freshmen? No. Hostel seats are limited, allocated on first-come first-served after admission. Many students live in private accommodation nearby (I-8, G-10). Apply for hostel via student portal after admission confirmed.

question: What are the names of hostels at NUST?
answer: NUST H-12 campus hostels: Boys (UG): Ghazali, Beruni, Razi, Rahmat, Attar, Liaquat, Hajveri, Zakariya. Boys (PG): Rumi, Johar, Ammar. Girls (UG): Zainab, Ayesha, Khadija, Amna. Girls (PG): Fatima. Room types: Single with attached bath, Double with attached bath, Double with community bath, Triple with community bath.
content: NUST hostel names: Boys UG hostels: Ghazali, Beruni, Razi, Rahmat, Attar, Liaquat, Hajveri, Zakariya. Boys PG: Rumi, Johar, Ammar. Girls UG: Zainab, Ayesha, Khadija, Amna. Girls PG: Fatima.

question: Is electricity bill included in hostel charges?
answer: Yes, for standard room occupancy the electricity and water bills are included in NUST hostel charges. However, if you use an air conditioner or electric heater, additional charges apply based on usage (metered separately). The security deposit (Rs. 15,000) covers damage and is refundable on departure.
content: Is electricity included in NUST hostel? Yes, standard electricity and water are included. AC/heater usage is metered and charged separately. Security deposit Rs. 15,000 is refundable.

question: Is transport (pick and drop) available for students? What are the charges?
answer: Yes, NUST provides bus pick and drop service for students living in Rawalpindi and Islamabad. Routes cover major areas. Approximate charges: Rs. 10,000–15,000 per semester depending on route. Detail of routes and charges are announced at orientation and available from the Student Affairs Directorate. No transport is provided outside Islamabad/Rawalpindi.
content: NUST transport/bus pick and drop: Available for Rawalpindi and Islamabad residents. Charges approx Rs. 10,000-15,000/semester depending on route. Details from Student Affairs Directorate.

question: Can I apply for migration to NUST from another university?
answer: Yes, NUST accepts migration applications from students at HEC-recognized universities. Requirements: Minimum CGPA 3.0/4.0. Must have completed at least 1 full year at the original university. Not allowed during first year or final year at NUST. Must complete minimum 60% of degree credit hours at NUST. Processing fee: Rs. 7,000. Additional fees: Rs. 100,000 (from local university) or Rs. 250,000 (from foreign university). Seats are limited and admission is at NUST's discretion.
content: Migration to NUST: Requires CGPA 3.0+, 1 year at original HEC-recognized university. Min 60% credits must be at NUST. Processing fee Rs. 7,000. Local transfer fee Rs. 100,000, foreign Rs. 250,000. Not allowed in first/final year.

question: How can I apply for MS or PhD at NUST?
answer: NUST postgraduate admissions (MS, MPhil, MBA, PhD) are handled separately from UG admissions. Apply at pgadmissions.nust.edu.pk. For MS/MPhil: Need GAT-General (NTS) or GRE or G-NET score with minimum 50-60% (varies by program). For PhD: MS/MPhil degree required. Test fee: Rs. 10,000. For MBA: GMAT/GRE or NUST-GAT. Contact: pgadmissions@nust.edu.pk. I am primarily designed for UG admission queries.
content: NUST MS/PhD admissions: Apply at pgadmissions.nust.edu.pk. MS needs GAT-General or GRE or G-NET min 50-60%. Application fee Rs. 10,000. PhD needs MS degree. Contact pgadmissions@nust.edu.pk.

question: Can gap year or repeater students apply to NUST?
answer: Yes, gap year and repeater candidates are fully eligible to apply to NUST. There is no age limit or penalty for appearing in admission after a gap year. Repeaters (those who have appeared for FSc/A-Level before) are treated equally with fresh candidates. Only your NET score, HSSC %, and SSC % matter for merit — not the year you appeared.
content: Can repeaters or gap year students apply to NUST? Yes. No age limit, no penalty for gap year. Repeaters treated equally. Only NET score, HSSC%, and SSC% matter for merit.

question: What if my A-Level result is not yet out when I apply?
answer: A-Level students whose results have not yet been announced can still apply to NUST. During the online application, indicate that your A-Level result is awaited. You must provide a provisional result or predicted grades letter from your school. Your IBCC equivalence for O-Levels is still required immediately. The A-Level IBCC equivalence must be provided before program commencement in September. Merit is calculated once results are confirmed.
content: A-Level result not out during NUST application? You can still apply — indicate result awaited. Provide school letter. O-Level IBCC still needed immediately. A-Level IBCC equivalence must be submitted before classes start in September.

question: What is the condensed math course at NUST?
answer: NUST offers a Condensed Mathematics course for students enrolled in Computing programs (BSCS, BSSE, BSAI) who come from Pre-Medical or non-Mathematics backgrounds. It is a non-credit remedial course of about 6 credit hours, typically run in the summer before or during 1st semester. Successfully completing it fulfills the Mathematics deficiency requirement and allows students to continue in their program without academic penalty.
content: NUST condensed math course: Non-credit remedial maths for Pre-Medical/non-Maths students in Computing programs (BSCS/BSSE/BSAI). Run before/during 1st semester. ~6 credit hours. Completing it removes Maths deficiency.

question: What is S3H at NUST and what programs does it offer?
answer: S3H (School of Social Sciences and Humanities) is NUST's school for social sciences, humanities, and law. Located at H-12 Islamabad campus. UG programs: BS Economics, BS Mass Communication & Media, BS Psychology, BS Governance & Public Policy, LLB (Law), BS Tourism & Hospitality Management. Fee: Rs. 250,380/semester (LLB also has Bar Council fee of Rs. 3,000). Admission via NET for Business Studies & Social Sciences pattern.
content: What is NUST S3H? School of Social Sciences and Humanities at H-12 Islamabad. Programs: BS Economics, BS Mass Comm, BS Psychology, BS Public Policy, LLB, BS Tourism. Fee Rs. 250,380/semester.

question: Can I change my program after admission?
answer: Program change within NUST is possible but limited. After 1st semester, students with high CGPA may apply for intra-institution transfer (within the same school, if allowed). Inter-institution transfer (changing school, e.g. from SEECS to NBS) is very rare and requires approval from both institutions plus the Registrar's office. Not all programs allow this. Consult your institution's academic office for current rules.
content: Program change after NUST admission: Intra-institution transfer possible after 1st semester with high CGPA. Inter-institution transfer very rare, needs both schools' approval. Consult academic office.

question: What is the fee for BS Accounting and Finance at NUST?
answer: BS Accounting and Finance (ACF) at NUST NBS has a tuition fee of Rs. 210,000 per semester for national students. Additionally: one-time Admission Processing Fee Rs. 35,000, Security Deposit Rs. 10,000 (refundable), and Miscellaneous dues Rs. 2,700/semester. International students pay USD 5,400 per annum.
content: Fee for BS Accounting and Finance (ACF) at NUST: Rs. 210,000/semester (national). Admission fee Rs. 35,000 (one-time). Security deposit Rs. 10,000 (refundable). International: USD 5,400/year.

question: What happens if I miss my NET session?
answer: If you miss your registered NET session, you will NOT get a refund of the Rs. 5,000 registration fee — it is non-refundable. However, you can register for and appear in a subsequent NET series (e.g., if you miss Series 2, you can still register for Series 3 or 4). NUST uses your best NET score across all series, so appearing in later sessions is still beneficial.
content: What if I miss my NET session? The Rs. 5,000 fee is non-refundable. You can still register for a later NET series. Best score across all attempts is considered — so appearing in later series is still valid.

question: Are there any quota / reserved seats?
answer: There are no quota seats available at NUST. All admissions are based purely on merit.

question: Are there any reserved / quota seats for admission in MBBS Programme?
answer: Admissions in NSHS for MBBS will be purely on merit, based upon the MDCAT conducted by NUMS.

question: Can a candidate apply both for open merit and ACT based seats?
answer: Yes, on the basis of NET / MDCAT Score and required ACT Score.

question: Can a candidate of ICS / equivalent qualification eligible to apply for Engineering programmes?
answer: Yes, candidates having combination of Physics, Mathematics and Computer Studies / Computer Science (ICS) or equivalent are eligible to apply for ALL Engineering programs offered by NUST, but they have to clear Chemistry as a remedial subject / course in the 1st Semester after admission.

question: Can candidates of FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification apply for BS Computer Science?
answer: Yes, but they have to clear deficient Mathematics courses of 6 credit hours, within one year of enrollment in the programme.

question: Can I apply for rechecking of Entry Test Result?
answer: Re-checking of Paper Based Entry Test may be requested within 5 days of the declaration of result along with a fee of Rs.500/- in the form of a bank draft/pay order in favour of NUST.

question: Does NUST cater for the pick and drop facility?
answer: Facility is available for the residents of Rawalpindi and Islamabad with specified routes, for which they have to pay separate charges. Details will be shared during orientation.

question: Is migration to NUST allowed?
answer: Yes, as per migration policy, students can apply for migration after completion of first year and before the beginning of final year. Applicants with exceptionally good academic record are considered.

question: Wait, what is the admission criteria for a gap year / repeater candidates?
answer: Gap year / repeater candidates are considered as normal candidates and there is no penalization while generating the merit.

question: Is the admission processing fee refundable?
answer: Admission Processing Fee is neither refundable nor transferable. It remains valid only for the series for which it has been paid.
